data.title: Eaton's CEO to speak at Morgan Stanley 13th Annual Laguna Conference on Sep 11 at 2:35 p.m. PT, discussing growth strategy.
data.description: Dublin-based Eaton (NYSE:ETN), specializing in intelligent power management, disclosed that its CEO, Paulo Ruiz, is set to engage in a fireside chat at Morgan Stanley’s 13th Annual Laguna Conference on September 11, 2025, at 2:35 p.m. PT. During the discussion, Ruiz will address investors on the corporation’s growth tactics in significant sectors like data centers, utilities, and aerospace. The session will be streamed live on the company’s Investor Relations platform.

data.title: Eric Trump predicts Bitcoin hitting $1 million and highlights global adoption, with nation states, Fortune 500 companies, and powerful families investing in BTC.
data.description: At a Bitcoin Asia conference in Hong Kong, Eric Trump, the son of U.S. President Donald Trump, highlighted the increasing global adoption of Bitcoin (BTC). He emphasized that governments and Fortune 500 companies are heavily investing in Bitcoin, stating that nation states and major corporations are actively pursuing the digital asset. Trump's remarks were made during a discussion with event organizer David Bailey, where he emphasized the widespread belief in Bitcoin as a digital store of value. The enthusiasm for Bitcoin is evident among the largest families and companies worldwide, as the momentum for Bitcoin acquisition continues to grow.

data.title: Vince Holdings to report Q2 2025 financial results on Sep 10 and conduct conference call at 4:30 p.m. ET.
data.description: Vince Holding Corp., a leading global contemporary retailer listed on the NYSE as VNCE, has revealed that it will disclose its financial performance for the second quarter of 2025 after the market closes on September 10, 2025. The Company is also scheduled to host a conference call at 4:30 p.m. Eastern Time on the same day. This call will provide an opportunity for stakeholders to inquire about business updates, financial trends, and other pertinent information.

data.title: Laurentian Bank of Canada reports Q3 2025 net income of $37.5M, EPS $0.73, ROE 5.0%. Adjusted net income $39.6M, EPS $0.78.
data.description: The Laurentian Bank of Canada, in Montreal, reported its financial results for the third quarter of 2025. The bank's net income for this quarter was $37.5 million with diluted earnings per share of $0.73, compared to $34.1 million and $0.67 for the same period in 2024. The return on common shareholders' equity was 5.0% in Q3 2025, up from 4.7% in Q3 2024. Adjusted net income was $39.6 million with adjusted diluted earnings per share of $0.78. For the nine months ended July 31, 2025, reported net income was $108.4 million compared to a net loss of $46.2 million in 2024. Diluted earnings per share for the nine-month period were $2.17. Adjusted net income for the same period was $113.0 million with adjusted diluted earnings per share of $2.28. Adjusted return on common shareholders' equity for the nine months was 5.3%. The bank's President and CEO, Éric Provost, attributed the bank's strong performance to its market position, risk management, liquidity, and capital strength, as well as the team's commitment and strategic execution.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson discontinues rheumatoid arthritis drug combo program after Phase 2a study with nipocalimab and anti-TNFα therapy. RA causes pain and swelling in joints. J&J spent $6.5B to acquire Momenta Pharmaceuticals for nipocalimab in 2020.
data.description: Johnson & Johnson recently disclosed preliminary results from the Phase 2a DAISY study, which explored the potential of combining nipocalimab with an anti-TNFα therapy in rheumatoid arthritis patients with refractory disease. Rheumatoid arthritis is a chronic autoimmune condition that triggers joint inflammation and pain, primarily affecting the hands and wrists on both sides of the body. In 2020, Johnson & Johnson invested a substantial $6.5 billion in acquiring Momenta Pharmaceuticals, allowing them to access nipocalimab, the company's flagship product. Notably, Johnson & Johnson also pledged $2 billion towards expanding US manufacturing operations amidst concerns about potential drug tariffs.
